response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack engineer with genuine systems experience (low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests per second, Kubernetes deployments), but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. C++ experience is limited to a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments and a thesis project, far short of the required 8 years, and his career centers on web/product development in TypeScript and Node.js rather than cloud compute platforms. There is no formal technical-leadership role on record; mentoring evidence is limited to workshops and community teaching.
strengths: Real high-throughput distributed-systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (async I/O, caching, low-latency services)
Broad, adaptable engineering background across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with strong testing/TDD craftsmanship
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing and mentoring inclination through workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ requirement (under 1 year of professional C++)
No formal technical-lead or people-leadership role; mentoring experience is informal
Limited production cloud-architecture depth for a compute-platform role (experience skews to product/full-stack work, AWS CDK exposure is narrow)

key_factors.factor: Lack of formal technical-leadership track record
